e2e-test-execution.desc: Developer runs `npm test`: Vitest loads config, global setup builds the fixture graph, test files exercise every CLI command, teardown cleans output

steps.label: discovers test files, registers global setup
builds fixture graph once via tskb build
provides CLI runners and graph loader to all test files
teardown cleans .tskb/ output directory

init-scaffolding-test.desc: `init.test.ts` runs: copies fixture to a temp dir, invokes `tskb init`, asserts the generated files

steps.label: orchestrates the test: arranges temp dir, runs init, asserts output
copies fixture src/ into the temp directory
runs tskb init --yes in the temp directory
asserts scaffolded files: tsconfig, starter doc, scripts, AI dirs

build-pipeline.desc: The tskb build process: source files through extraction to knowledge graph outputs

steps.label: Orchestrates the pipeline: file discovery, extraction, graph building, output generation
Scans TypeScript AST for global tskb namespace declarations (Folders, Modules, Exports, Terms)
Receives completed registry; traverses JSX trees to extract content, references, relations, and flows — embeds data-node-display in reference links using registry-resolved paths
Merges registry and docs into a unified graph with nodes, edges, and hierarchy
Writes the graph to .tskb/graph/ — one JSON file per node type plus a search index

graph-to-chunks-transform.desc: The explorer asks transformGraph to chunk a KnowledgeGraph for delivery: edges are indexed, the meta chunk is built, folder chunks are walked, ghosts are filled in, then counts and ordering are finalised

steps.label: entry point: instantiates the transformer, indexes edges by source and target, then runs the phases below
builds the meta chunk: root folder, top folders, docs, flows, terms, externals, and cross-lane edges
recursively builds one folder chunk per folder with its direct modules, exports, sub-folders, and import edges (split into internal vs external)
fills in ghost intermediary folders for path gaps between a declared folder and its modules, then injects ghost nodes for undeclared files and folders found by the scanner
patches folder child counts so the SPA can show expand affordances, sorts chunk contents alphabetically, and builds the parent-of map

router-setup.desc: Boot wiring: panel shell subscribes, view factory is registered for hash restore, hash sync starts

steps.label: ExplorerApp.mount() runs the panel-router setup block after canvas and renderer are ready
new DocPanel(router): shell subscribes so every stack change re-renders the panel
registerView(RefsView, deps): binds deps into a factory closure keyed by RefsView.prefix
init({ syncHash: true }): starts hashchange listening and restores any view in the current hash
If location.hash is non-empty, restoreFromHash() calls the factory to rebuild the view
onViewChange fires; header and body are rendered by the restored view

router-push.desc: Forward navigation: a chip click constructs a view, pushes it, the shell renders it, and the URL hash is updated

steps.label: onChipClick(node, 'docs' | 'flows'): constructs new RefsView(nodeId, kind, deps)
router.push(view): identical-route guard skips dupes; otherwise appends to the stack
notify() fans out the new top-of-stack and canGoBack to every subscriber
onViewChange clears title/body, calls view.renderHeader(title, ctx) then view.renderBody(body)
renderHeader and renderBody write HTML then call wireRefs on their own elements
writeHash() writes #/<route> to location.hash; the writingHash flag suppresses the echoed hashchange

router-hash-restore.desc: Browser back/forward or external hash change rebuilds the view from the URL

steps.label: hashchange fires; if writingHash is set the event is our own echo and is ignored
restoreFromHash() strips the leading '#/'; an empty hash empties the stack and notifies
parseRoute(raw) splits the prefix, looks up the registered factory, and calls it with the rest
RefsView.parse(rest, deps) decodes the route and returns a fresh view instance (or null for malformed routes)
Existing stack is drained (each onLeave fired) and replaced with the single restored view
onViewChange paints the restored view; getNode may return undefined if the chunk hasn't loaded yet

router-refresh-after-load.desc: A hash-restored view that painted with placeholder labels repaints once the meta chunk arrives

steps.label: loadInitialData() awaits the meta chunk and writes it to the store
router.refresh() re-notifies subscribers without changing the stack
notify() fans out the same top-of-stack view to every subscriber
onViewChange runs again; the view re-queries deps.getNode() and now resolves real labels

explorer-serve-flow.desc: Developer runs `tskb explore`: the CLI loads the graph, transforms it into chunks, starts an HTTP server, and the browser loads the SPA and fetches chunks on demand

steps.label: loads the graph file and routes to the serve path
converts the KnowledgeGraph into a MetaChunk and one FolderChunk per folder, held in memory
starts the HTTP server: chunk routes serve the in-memory chunks as JSON; everything else serves the SPA assets
ExplorerApp mounts on page load: sets up canvas, renderer, and search, then fetches the meta chunk
positions all visible nodes across the three lanes
D3 enter/update renders node cards into SVG groups

explorer-export-flow.desc: Developer runs the explorer export command: the graph file is read, chunks are transformed in memory, then the pre-built SPA and chunk files are written to the output directory

steps.label: reads the graph file and resolves the output directory from the export flag
converts KnowledgeGraph into a MetaChunk plus one FolderChunk per folder, held in memory
copies the pre-built SPA into the output directory and writes all chunk JSON files alongside it

explorer-app-boot.desc: ExplorerApp.mount(): four setup phases then meta fetch that triggers the first render

steps.label: new ExplorerApp().mount() called on page load
Appends zoom-layer → lane-bg-layer / boundary-layer / edge-layer / node-layer; configures d3.zoom with tooltip transform propagation
mountNodeTooltip() and mountCodeTooltip() attach DOM tooltip elements anchored to the SVG
createNodeRenderer() wires onExpand / onSelect / onTraceLinks / hasChildren / code preview callbacks to ExplorerApp instance methods
Creates search Web Worker; button click posts query to worker; worker results update matchIds and call render()
loader.load('meta') fetches /chunks/meta.json; global spinner shown while pending
store.loadMeta() stores the chunk and notifies the render subscriber
First render(): computeLayout positions top-folder nodes across the Structure lane

explorer-app-render.desc: A store update or interaction calls ExplorerApp.render(): layout, edges, D3 join, search dim

steps.label: triggered by store subscription or a direct call from an interaction handler
computeLayout(store, expanded): builds d3.hierarchy from visible tree, runs d3.tree for left-to-right positions. Result cached until layoutDirty is set.
computeRenderState(store, layout, matchIds): derives allNodes, canvasW, structureLinks, relationLinks. matchIds comes from the search worker. Pure — no D3.
renderLaneBands(): draws labeled background bands for Structure and Externals lanes
buildStructureLinks(structureNodes): derives parent→child StructureLink pairs from parentId fields
renderStructureEdges(): redraws cubic-bezier SVG paths; ghost links get dashed stroke
D3 enter/update/exit: new node cards appended, merged nodes repositioned, removed nodes fade out; search dim sets 0.15 on non-matching nodes

explorer-app-expand.desc: ExplorerApp.onExpand(): fetch chunk if needed, update expanded set, re-render

steps.label: User clicks + button; BaseNodeRenderer fires the onExpand callback
If already expanded: collapseDescendants() clears the subtree, calls render()
If chunk not cached: showNodeSpinner() appends animated SVG ring next to the node
loader.load('folder', id): cache hit or fetches /chunks/folder-{sanitizedId}.json
removeNodeSpinner() called in finally — always removes spinner whether fetch succeeded or failed
store.loadFolderChunk() stores chunk and notifies subscribers
render() called after expanded set update — new nodes enter, layout recomputed

static-analysis.desc: `tskb build` invokes createProgram to set up TypeScript static analysis, then hands the Program to extraction and graph assembly

steps.label: creates Program from tsconfig + source files
walks AST for Folders, Modules, Terms, Exports
parses JSX trees for content and references
assembles nodes and edges into knowledge graph

module-morphology-extraction.desc: During `tskb build`, extractRegistry enriches every Module declaration with its public API: exports, imports, and type stubs

steps.label: collects Module declarations from tskb namespace
resolves source file, classifies exports, renders code stubs
walks import declarations for symbols and paths
assembles enriched ModuleNode with edges

docs-extraction-pipeline.desc: Single .tskb.tsx file through extraction: constant map → JSX traversal → HTML + references

steps.label: Iterates Program source files; dispatches matched .tsx files to extractFromTsxFile
Builds constant ref map, finds default export, instantiates JsxExtractor
Walks all variable declarations; maps const names → {category, name} via type-assertion parsing
Passed in as optional param; queried by buildRefLink to pre-compute data-node-display values
Depth-first JSX traversal via visitElement dispatch: structural tags → HTML, XxxRef / inline refs → buildRefLink; Flow and Relation elements validated and collected
Emits <a class=tskb-ref data-node-id data-node-type data-node-display>; pushes id into refs sub-array
Called per Flow step: probes all registry maps to return {nodeType, display} for the step anchor
Returned: filePath, HTML content, references arrays, flows[], relations[]
Consumes ExtractedDoc: creates DocNode + FlowNode entries and references/flow-step edges
